overview: Original Divine Chamber uploads live here as atmosphere, pressure, and recurring cues beside the written archive.

collectionNotes.Featured: Lead with the cue that best introduces the project's atmosphere before a reader opens the archive.
collectionNotes.From the Chamber: Use these shelves for interior, theatrical, intimate, or symbolic pieces.
collectionNotes.From the Bureau: Keep investigation, pressure, city movement, and case-facing tracks here.
collectionNotes.Motifs & Interludes: Smaller cues, recurring relationship motifs, and transitional pieces can live here.

tracks.title: Featured Cue
tracks.summary: Use this slot for the first Divine Chamber upload new visitors should hear.
tracks.note: This featured player is designed for one strong cue rather than a dense wall of embeds.

tracks.title: Chamber Cue
tracks.summary: Reserve this shelf for music that feels interior, charged, witty, or symbolically alive.
tracks.note: A chamber-facing cue pairs well with dossiers, scenes, and relationship pressure.

tracks.title: Bureau Cue
tracks.summary: Use this shelf for tracks tied to fieldwork, case escalation, or official pressure.
tracks.note: Bureau pieces can carry more movement and tension without losing the archive's restraint.

tracks.title: City Pressure Motif
tracks.summary: A smaller cue for the city itself, institutional tension, or the sensation of pressure building.
tracks.note: This shelf works well for transitional or environmental tracks.

tracks.title: Lateumn Interlude
tracks.summary: Keep quieter or reflective uploads here when they deepen the archive without overpowering it.
tracks.note: Interludes can act like breath marks between heavier case or chamber material.
